title: phlegmariurus dacrydioides
intro: Phlegmariurus dacrydioides, also known as the "weeping spleenwort", is a type of terrestrial fern that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus dacrydioides is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
